Ad Text:
About FinXL IT

FinXL IT Professional Services is a leading IT consultancy that partners with Tier-1 clients to bridge the gap between business needs and technology.

About this Opportunity

FinXL IT is seeking a Senior Data Integration Engineer (Level 5) to join our team at a leading financial services organisation. This is a high-visibility role designed for a technical heavyweight who excels at the intersection of streaming architecture and enterprise integration.

As a "technical linchpin," you will be responsible for moving beyond traditional batch processing to design and implement sophisticated, event-driven data architectures for systems of record.

Key Responsibilities


Real-Time Architecture: Design and deploy advanced event-driven integration patterns to enable near-instantaneous data responses across global P&C platforms.

Streaming Mastery: Act as the SME for Confluent Kafka, managing complex message schemas and ensuring robust, seamless stream processing.

Strategic Leadership: Author technical options papers for the Architecture Board and articulate complex technical benefits to senior non-technical sponsors.

Technical Governance: Lead deep-dive code reviews and mentor junior engineers, ensuring the highest standards of engineering excellence.

Documentation: Maintain world-class technical documentation and architectural diagrams within Confluence to drive transparency and knowledge sharing.


Requirements


Streaming & Messaging: Extensive hands-on experience with Confluent Kafka is essential.

Architectural Depth: A deep, "under-the-hood" understanding of Event-Driven Architecture (EDA) and integration patterns.

Governance: Experience navigating complex risk and compliance frameworks within a Banking or BFSI environment.

Communication: Ability to translate complex people-data requirements into elegant technical solutions for both engineering teams and business stakeholders.

Tooling: Proficiency in Confluence for documenting operational playbooks and technical designs.


What's On Offer?


Initial 12 months' contract, with a view to extend
Located in Sydney CBD
Hybrid work mode to get your best work in! (2 days in the office and 3 days remote)

Why This Role?

This isn't just a "developer" role; it is a Level 5 Senior Contributor position. You will have a seat at the table with the Architecture Board and the autonomy to drive the evolution of data integration capabilities for one of the world's leading financial groups.

Next Steps

If you are an integration specialist who thrives on solving complex data challenges, please apply now via the link.

Job number (S4)

The Role

We are seeking a skilled Data Engineer to join our data team and help design, build, and maintain our data infrastructure and analytics pipelines. You'll work with modern data tools including Databricks, Amazon Quick Suite, and DBT to deliver high-quality data solutions that drive business insights and decision-making. 

Open for Brisbane and Sunshine Coast candidates.

About FlowLogic

At FlowLogic, we’re not just building software; we’re shaping the future of care through innovation. As a fast-growing company in the technology and services space, we’re a leading cloud-based solution designed to transform how NDIS providers deliver support across ANZ. Our platform empowers disability organisations to streamline operations, boost productivity, and stay fully compliant, freeing them to focus on delivering life-changing services.

Behind our success is a close-knit, high-performing team with a strong culture of collaboration, curiosity, and continuous growth. You’ll work directly with senior leaders, have a clear impact from day one, and enjoy the support you need to grow your career in a business that’s scaling fast and going places.

Your Responsibilities

Reporting directly to the Head of Engineering, you will:

Design, build, and maintain scalable data pipelines using Databricks and DBT for data transformation and modelling.
Develop and optimise data warehouse architecture, ensuring data quality, reliability, and performance.
Create and maintain dashboards and reports using Amazon Quick Suite to deliver actionable insights to stakeholders.
Design and implement dimensional models, star schemas, and other data structures using DBT best practices.
Build efficient ETL/ELT workflows to integrate data from multiple sources into our data lake and warehouse.
Implement data validation, testing, and monitoring frameworks to ensure data accuracy and consistency.
Optimise queries, data models, and pipeline performance in Databricks and related systems.
Partner with data analysts, data scientists, and business teams to understand requirements and deliver data solutions.
Create and maintain comprehensive documentation for data models, pipelines, and processes.
Establish and promote data engineering best practices, coding standards, and version control workflows.
Debug and resolve data pipeline issues and performance bottlenecks.

Essential Criteria

2+ years of hands-on experience with Databricks, including Spark, Delta Lake, and notebook development.
Strong proficiency in DBT for data transformation, including models, tests, documentation, and deployment.
Experience building interactive dashboards, visualisations, and reports using Amazon Quick Suite.
Solid experience with AWS services (Athena, S3, Redshift, Glue, Lambda, Athena, IAM).
Advanced SQL skills for complex queries, optimisation, and database design.
Proficiency in Python and/or Scala for data processing and automation.
Strong understanding of data warehouse concepts, dimensional modelling, and star/snowflake schemas.
Experience with Git and modern software development practices (CI/CD, code reviews, testing).
Familiarity with distributed computing concepts and large-scale data processing.
Experience working with various data formats (Parquet, JSON, CSV, Avro).

Advantageous

Experience with other BI tools (Tableau, Power BI, Looker).
Knowledge of Apache Airflow or other workflow orchestration tools.
Experience with data governance and data cataloguing tools.
Familiarity with machine learning workflows and MLOps.
Understanding of data security, compliance, and privacy regulations (GDPR, CCPA).
Experience with streaming data platforms (Kafka, Kinesis).
AWS certifications (Data Analytics, Solutions Architect, etc.).
Experience with Snowflake or other cloud data warehouses.
Knowledge of data quality tools (Great Expectations, Monte Carlo).

What We Offer

A supportive, inclusive, and engaged team environment.
The opportunity to do meaningful work at the intersection of care and technology.
A competitive salary package, with potential for growth and development.

How to apply

We appreciate all candidates who have taken the time to apply, but only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

We value diversity, and we believe that our uniqueness makes us stronger. We encourage applicants from diverse and/or minority backgrounds to express their interest. We believe in creating a working environment where individuals are able to bring their full selves to work and are valued for their unique contributions.

Job Title:  Data Engineer
Location: Sydney, Australia (Hybrid)
Job Type: Permanent, full-time

About the Role:
Our client is looking for an experienced Data Engineer to join their growing data function and help ensure the organisation’s data pipelines, integrations, and reporting assets are reliable, accurate, and performing at their best.

This is a permanent, full-time position within a data-driven business where analytics sits at the heart of strategy. You’ll be working with critical data infrastructure across digital marketing, web analytics, and enterprise reporting.

What you’ll do:

Build, maintain, and optimise end-to-end data pipelines using Databricks and dbt.
Integrate data from third-party sources including Google Analytics, Meta campaign data, and other digital marketing platforms via APIs.
Manage API integrations and third-party data source connections, ensuring data quality and consistency.
Collaborate with teams across data engineering and analytics to deliver trusted, high-quality data assets for reporting and analytics.
What we’re looking for:

2–5+ years of experience in a Data Engineering role.
Solid hands-on experience with Databricks and dbt.
Strong proficiency in Python and SQL.
Experience working with cloud-based data platforms.
Exposure to digital marketing data sources such as Google Analytics, Meta Ads, and similar platforms.
Practical experience with API integrations and managing third-party data pipelines.
Strong communication skills with the ability to work effectively across technical and non-technical teams.
What’s in it for you?

Work with a highly data-driven organisation where analytics is central to decision-making.
Competitive salary package with employee benefits (wellness programs, learning & development programs, retail discounts). 
Hands-on access to modern data tools and technologies including Databricks, dbt, BigQuery, and Python.
Collaborative team environment where your contributions have real impact.
This role offers hybrid flexibility with 3 days in the office. You must be based in Sydney and have full working rights in Australia.

How to Apply
If you’re a Data Engineer with strong experience across Databricks, dbt, and cloud-based data platforms, we’d love to hear from you. Apply now or reach out to us at hello@algotalent.ai for a confidential conversation.

Know someone perfect for the role?
We offer $1,000 referral bonuses for successful placements. Refer a friend or colleague by DMing us or contacting hello@algotalent.ai

Hello. We’re Virgin Australia. We’re the airline that’s always done things a little different. Our way. The Virgin way. We’re on a mission to make flying better than ‘nice’. And to do that, it takes a team of talented people who are passionate about customer experience, doing the right thing always, with a focus on safety – in the air, on the ground and in our workplaces. A team who values real diversity and inclusion, in a big brand with big ambitions. 

With millions of customer interactions across our ecosystem and a uniquely rich dataset spanning customer behaviour, travel and partner engagement, data plays a central role in how we personalise experiences and make decisions at scale.

About the role:

We’re looking for a Data Scientist to join our Advanced Analytics Centre of Excellence. In this role, you’ll build, deploy and scale machine learning and experimentation solutions that directly shape loyalty strategy, customer decisioning and personalisation, partner performance, and customer experience across the Velocity ecosystem.

You’ll work in a cross functional environment alongside Insight Analysts, Analytics Engineers and business stakeholders, owning problems end to end from framing and experiment design through to production deployment and measurable business impact, embedding models into production systems that power large scale customer and member decisioning.

You’ll report to the Manager, Personalisation Analytics and Data Science and join a team with established models in production, including recommender systems, propensity and regression models, supported by an evolving MLOps framework built on Databricks and AutoML. These models are actively used to power large scale member decisioning across the Velocity program.

As Velocity accelerates its AI transformation journey, you’ll partner closely with our AI teams to identify, develop and embed AI driven capabilities into scalable products and decisioning frameworks.

In this role you will:

Design, build and deploy statistical and machine learning models using Python and Spark within a modern, scalable data and ML platform, including Databricks, with a focus on deploying models into production environments supporting batch and increasingly near real time decisioning
Own the end to end lifecycle of models, from problem framing and feature engineering through to deployment, monitoring and optimisation
Develop and enhance recommender systems, propensity models and predictive models that drive commercial outcomes
Design and implement experiments (A/B and beyond) to inform decision making, measure causal impact and continuously optimise outcomes
Contribute to and improve MLOps practices, including automation, reproducibility, scalable deployment pipelines and model monitoring
Build scalable data products and reusable decisioning frameworks
Translate complex analyses into clear, actionable insights using effective data visualisation and storytelling for both technical and non technical audiences
Partner closely with analytics, engineering and business teams to embed data-driven decision making into workflows
Ensure all work aligns with Virgin Australia Group’s safety, security and resilience standards

You’ll be great in this role if you have:

A degree in a quantitative discipline such as Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics or Computer Science (PhD desirable)
Around 3+ years of experience building and deploying machine learning models in a commercial environment
Proficiency in Python, with solid SQL skills and experience working with large datasets (Spark desirable)
Experience working with modern data and ML platforms such as Databricks and/or AWS
A strong foundation in statistics, experimental design and ideally causal inference
Experience working within or contributing to MLOps frameworks
A track record of translating models and analysis into measurable business impact
Strong communication and data visualisation skills, with the ability to influence stakeholders and drive data-driven decisions
Curiosity, ownership and a drive to automate and scale solutions
Experience in loyalty, travel or consumer products (nice to have)

Why this role is interesting:

Work on production grade machine learning systems that power decisioning at scale
Build on an existing ML and MLOps ecosystem with modern tooling (Databricks, AutoML)
Help shape how experimentation, personalisation and AI driven decisioning are scaled across Velocity

Candidates must be Sydney based, or willing to relocate.

What you’ll get from us 

We’re committed to looking after you, with some of the best benefits and conditions in the industry – including (but not limited to): 

Discounted flights for you and your loved ones (plus $1000 travel credits per year) 
Flexible and hybrid working, with a mix of working from home and the office 
‘Dress for Your Day’ – wear whatever makes you feel great for the type of work you do and the day you have ahead of you 
Discounts on travel insurance, lounge membership, car hire, accommodation, and experiences worldwide 
Cheaper hospitality, retail, technology, beauty and wellness services 
Free counselling and online tools to support your physical, mental, social and financial health. 

When everyone is included, everyone wins 

We’re all individuals. And we love that. That’s why we encourage applications from Indigenous Australians, people with disability, those from diverse cultural backgrounds, our friends in the LGBTIQ+ community, and anyone else who wants to join us. 

Ready to apply? 

Working at Virgin Australia isn't just a job. It's a passion that becomes part of you. Are you ready to join us? Get ready for a rewarding experience. Apply today!

Ad Text:
Campus Location: North Sydney, Australia | Brisbane, Australia | Melbourne, Australia

Job No: APTAV128265#013

Utilise technical expertise and stakeholder engagement skills to deliver scalable, AI-enabled and automated digital solutions

Work with new team designing and building intelligent, enterprise-wide digital solutions to improve ACU's capability

ACU offers 17% employer superannuation, salary packaging and generous leave provisions

Two full time, permanent opportunities with hybrid flexibility working at North Sydney, Melbourne or Brisbane campus

About the role:

Operating within Digital Innovation & Change, the Solutions Engineer is active in the strategic design and implementation of digital solutions across the enterprise to align technological initiatives with ACU's strategy. Reporting to the National Manager - Digital Innovation & Change this role is responsible for contributing to technical direction, influencing digital policy and ensuring integration across the university's faculties and directorates nationally. The Solutions Engineer will form part of a new team and collaborate with cross-functional teams to design, develop and implement innovative solutions that drive business success. The position will work closely with stakeholders, faculties and business units along with the Information Technology Directorate to identify business requirements and translate them into technical solutions. Also, the role will require staying up to date with emerging technologies and industry trends to recommend and implement innovative solutions that align with university objectives.

For full position details, refer to the position description

Contact:

General enquiries can be sent to Brett Wiskar, National Manager - Digital Innovation & Change at: Brett.Wiskar@acu.edu.au

What we offer:

The University pursues an excellence agenda and offers an environment where staff are welcomed and safe, and valued through development, participation and involvement.

Total remuneration valued to $138,111 - $154,409 total rem (pro rata) pa, including salary component $116,706 - $130,478 (pro rata) pa (HEW 8), employer contribution to superannuation and annual leave loading.

17% employer superannuation contributions to eligible continuing and fixed-term staff.

Annual leave loading of an equivalent to 17.5% of four weeks' salary pro rata payable, dependent on your commencement date.

As an ACU staff member, you will have access to generous benefits which include but not limited to: flexible work, study leave, a variety of leave provisions and salary packaging. Find out more information about the benefits or working at ACU here.

How to Apply:

Step 1: Review the Position Description and become familiar with Our Mission and the Application process

Step 2: Click the 'View Position Description and Apply' button, and populate your candidate application details, including your:

Resume.

Recommended responses to the Selection Criteria and Core Competencies in a cover letter (maximum 2 pages) or in the online Declaration and Criteria application page.

Brief online responses to any pre-screening employment questions.

Applications close: 06-APR-2026 at 11:59pm AUS Eastern Standard Time

Preferred candidates will be required to complete mandatory pre-employment declarations and background checks, including those related to gender-based violence and foreign interference, in line with ACU's compliance requirements.

Candidates must hold Australian work rights to apply for this position. For permanent/ continuing roles, applicants must have permanent work rights. For further information visit Immigration and Citizenship(homeaffairs.gov.au)

Your Privacy of personal information is maintained in accordance with ACU policy. For more details visit: www.acu.edu.au/careers

ACU is committed to fostering an inclusive workplace culture that values diversity and enables our people to reach their potential. This is underpinned by our mission and values, grounded in our respect for all people and the advancement of the common good. In line with our commitment to equity, diversity and inclusion we welcome candidates from all backgrounds and lived experiences. ACU is recognised as a Veteran Employment Supporter and veterans are encouraged to apply. ACU offers support for candidates that may require adjustments through the recruitment process. Please contact recruitment@acu.edu.au to discuss your requirements in confidence.

About ACU:

Australian Catholic University (ACU) is an inclusive community which welcomes students and staff of all beliefs. ACU has over 2,500 staff supporting more than 34,000 students across eight campuses - Ballarat, Brisbane, Canberra, Melbourne, North Sydney, Strathfield, Blacktown and Rome.

ACU aspires to be a home for our staff, offer the care of a family and develop an academic and professional community. Whatever your faith or culture, and whether you are academic or professional staff, there are many opportunities both for learning and contributing at ACU.

As valued members of our community, all staff members are expected to have an understanding of ACU's mission and values and to demonstrate an active contribution to them.

ACU does not accept candidate applications submitted by external recruitment agencies through the ACU careers website.

Job Title: AI Adoption & Enablement Lead or AI Adoption SME
Ad Text:
The AI Adoption & Enablement Lead is responsible for driving organisation-wide adoption, effective usage, and measurable productivity outcomes from enterprise AI tools. The initial focus is on uplifting baseline adoption and competency across the business, followed by the development of role-specific and use‑case‑driven value, particularly for legal and finance users. This role plays a critical part in delivering the CEO-mandated objective of 100% AI adoption by the end of May.

Context

Organisation-wide AI programme currently run by a small, central team.

Primary AI tools in scope include Grok (default AI tool), Copilot (currently web chat), and ChatGPT Enterprise.

Current challenges include low adoption of AI tools, uneven confidence and competency in AI usage, and limited understanding of how to apply LLMs effectively in day-to-day work.

Core user groups include lawyers and finance professionals who are not necessarily technically savvy and require structured enablement.

Key Responsibilities

Adoption & Enablement

Drive organisation-wide adoption of AI tools, with Grok positioned as the default AI platform.

Design and deliver enablement programs that build confidence and practical competency in using AI, including:




How to use large language models (LLMs)

Prompting fundamentals and best practices

Responsible and effective use of AI in professional contexts

Actively support, facilitate, and where required directly run AI training sessions (e.g. workshops, drop-in sessions, roadshows) for individuals and teams.

Develop simple, repeatable education assets (training sessions, guides, examples, playbooks) tailored to non-technical users.

Ensure strong uptake and usage of AI tools already built and available within the business.

Productivity & Measurement

Shift focus from adoption-only metrics to productivity and outcome-based measurement.

Define and track task-based outcomes for individuals and teams.

Measure and report on key usage indicators, including:




Prompt quality

Time spent in AI tools

Hits, sessions, and frequency of use

Use data and insights to continuously refine enablement approaches and prioritise high-impact use cases.

Use Case Development

Initially prioritise broad adoption and baseline competency uplift across all staff.

Progressively identify and develop more specific, role-based and function-specific AI use cases.

Partner closely with legal and finance teams to understand how AI can best support their workflows and decision-making.

Maintain a strong focus on "how we help our users" rather than technology for its own sake.

Stakeholder & Programme Support

Work closely with the central AI programme team to scale impact despite limited resources.

Act as a trusted advisor to business leaders and end users on practical AI adoption.

Support leadership objectives and communicate progress clearly against the CEO’s adoption mandate.

Skills & Experience

Essential

Demonstrated experience driving technology adoption, enablement, or change across a broad user base.

Strong understanding of generative AI and LLMs, with the ability to explain concepts simply and practically.

Proven ability to design and deliver training or enablement for non-technical audiences.

Experience defining and tracking adoption, usage, and productivity metrics.

Highly self-directed and proactive, with the ability to operate effectively in a small team.

Desirable

Experience working with legal, finance, or other professional services users.

Exposure to enterprise AI platforms such as Copilot, ChatGPT Enterprise, or similar tools.

Background in digital transformation, change management, or capability uplift programs.

Personal Attributes

Self-starter who takes initiative and ownership, avoiding over-reliance on AI or excessive supervision.

Strong communicator who can build trust with non-technical stakeholders.

Outcome-focused, with a clear bias toward practical value and productivity.

Comfortable working in ambiguity and evolving an approach as adoption matures.

Success Measures

Achieving and sustaining near‑100% AI tool adoption across the organisation.

Measurable uplift in employee confidence and competency in using AI.

Clear evidence of productivity improvements tied to task-based outcomes.

Hello. We’re Virgin Australia. We’re the airline that’s always done things a little different. Our way. The Virgin way. We’re on a mission to make flying better than ‘nice’. And to do that, it takes a team of talented people who are passionate about customer experience, doing the right thing always, with a focus on safety – in the air, on the ground and in our workplaces. A team who values real diversity and inclusion, in a big brand with big ambitions. 

With millions of customer interactions across our ecosystem and a uniquely rich dataset spanning customer behaviour, travel and partner engagement, data plays a central role in how we personalise experiences and make decisions at scale.

 

About the role:

We’re looking for a Senior Data Scientist to join our Advanced Analytics Centre of Excellence. In this role, you’ll build, deploy and scale machine learning and experimentation solutions that directly shape loyalty strategy, customer decisioning and personalisation, partner performance, and customer experience across the Velocity ecosystem.

You’ll work in a cross functional environment alongside Insight Analysts, Analytics Engineers and business stakeholders, leading complex problem spaces end to end from framing and experiment design through to production deployment and measurable business impact, embedding models into production systems that power large scale customer and member decisioning.

You’ll report to the Manager, Personalisation Analytics and Data Science and join a team with established models in production, including recommender systems, propensity and regression models, supported by an evolving MLOps framework built on Databricks and AutoML. These models are actively used to power large scale customer and member decisioning across the Velocity program.

You’ll play a key role in shaping and owning the data science roadmap, working closely with the manager to prioritise initiatives and ensure delivery against commitments.

As Velocity accelerates its AI transformation journey, you’ll partner closely with our AI teams to identify, develop and embed AI driven capabilities into scalable products and decisioning frameworks.

 

In this role you will:

Drive the data science roadmap for your domain, identifying and prioritising high impact opportunities aligned to business objectives and ensuring delivery against agreed commitments
Design, build and deploy statistical and machine learning models using Python and Spark within a modern, scalable data and ML platform, including Databricks, with a focus on deploying models into production environments supporting batch and increasingly near real time decisioning
Lead the end to end lifecycle of models, from problem framing and feature engineering through to deployment, monitoring and optimisation
Develop and enhance recommender systems, propensity models and predictive models that drive commercial outcomes
Define and evolve experimentation frameworks (A/B and beyond) to inform decision making, measure causal impact and continuously optimise outcomes
Own the evolution of MLOps practices within data science, driving improvements in model deployment, monitoring and lifecycle management in partnership with engineering teams
Build scalable data products and reusable decisioning frameworks
Provide technical leadership and mentorship, raising the bar for data science across the team
Translate complex analyses into clear, actionable insights using effective data visualisation and storytelling for both technical and non technical audiences
Influence senior stakeholders and partner closely with analytics, engineering and business teams to embed data-driven decision making into workflows
Ensure all work aligns with Virgin Australia Group’s safety, security and resilience standards

 

You’ll be great in this role if you have:

A degree in a quantitative discipline such as Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics or Computer Science (PhD desirable)
Typically 5+ years of experience building and deploying machine learning models in a commercial environment
Proven experience leading end to end data science initiatives with measurable business impact
Proficiency in Python, with strong SQL skills and experience working with large and complex datasets (Spark desirable)
Experience working with modern data and ML platforms such as Databricks and/or AWS
A strong foundation in statistics, experimental design and causal inference
Experience shaping or working within MLOps frameworks and production ML environments
Strong communication and data visualisation skills, with the ability to influence senior stakeholders and drive data-driven decisions with measurable business impact
Demonstrated ability to operate in ambiguous environments and translate business problems into scalable data science solutions
Curiosity, ownership and a drive to automate and scale solutions
Experience in loyalty, travel or consumer products (nice to have)

 

Why this role is interesting:

Work on production grade machine learning systems that power customer decisioning at scale
Build on an existing ML and MLOps ecosystem with modern tooling (Databricks, AutoML)
Own and shape the data science roadmap and drive delivery of high impact initiatives
Help scale experimentation, personalisation and AI driven decisioning across Velocity

Candidates must be Sydney based, or willing to relocate.

 

What you’ll get from us 

We’re committed to looking after you, with some of the best benefits and conditions in the industry – including (but not limited to): 

Discounted flights for you and your loved ones (plus $1000 travel credits per year) 
Flexible and hybrid working, with a mix of working from home and the office 
‘Dress for Your Day’ – wear whatever makes you feel great for the type of work you do and the day you have ahead of you 
Discounts on travel insurance, lounge membership, car hire, accommodation, and experiences worldwide 
Cheaper hospitality, retail, technology, beauty and wellness services 
Free counselling and online tools to support your physical, mental, social and financial health. 

 

When everyone is included, everyone wins 

We’re all individuals. And we love that. That’s why we encourage applications from Indigenous Australians, people with disability, those from diverse cultural backgrounds, our friends in the LGBTIQ+ community, and anyone else who wants to join us. 

 

Ready to apply? 

Working at Virgin Australia isn't just a job. It's a passion that becomes part of you. Are you ready to join us? Get ready for a rewarding experience. Apply today!

Company description:



Accelerate AI adoption. Influence how we hire.

Established in 1840, we're Australia's first member-owned wellbeing company. Today we have over $1 billion in revenue and provide smart solutions and services to more than 700,000 Australians. We employ over 7000 people and our purpose is to Help People to Thrive.

It's an exciting time to be joining Australian Unity - we have grown significantly over recent years and are transforming to capitalise on further growth opportunities to help our customers and employees thrive. We operate with commercial principles and with a strong social purpose to create community value. Australian Unity is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.



Job description:



You will lead the end‑to‑end adoption of AI solutions within our People & Culture function - from identifying opportunities to delivering real business value. A major early focus will be partnering with our recruitment platform vendor to ensure their AI models meet our standards for fairness, transparency, performance, and compliance.

In this role, you will:

Lead end‑to‑end AI adoption within a business squad - from identifying opportunities and shaping requirements to solution selection, delivery, and post‑launch support.
Work directly with AI vendors - challenging assumptions, asking the right technical questions, evaluating model behaviour, ensuring fairness and performance, and influencing product direction.
Partner with internal technical and business stakeholders to shape use cases, drive adoption, build user confidence, and ensure AI solutions deliver measurable business value.
Ensure all AI initiatives meet governance, risk, and compliance expectations, and that solutions are well‑designed, documented, and aligned with enterprise standards.
Contribute to the evolution of enterprise‑level AI practices in an area such as AI literacy & change, AI governance, or AIOps / AI innovation.

You'll be part of a small, expert group helping Australian Unity become an AI‑native enterprise - and you'll have the freedom to shape how we get there.

About You:

We're looking for someone with a strong technical foundation who is ready to apply it in a more product‑oriented role. You may come from a software engineering or computer science background with hands‑on AI experience, and you've either already made the move into product-style work - or you're excited to take that next step.

You enjoy solving business problems, engaging with stakeholders, and challenging vendors with the right technical questions. You're as comfortable discussing model behaviour and risks as you are explaining concepts to non‑technical audiences.

To be successful in the role you will be able to demonstrate the following capabilities / experience:

Hands‑on industry experience (3+ years) in software engineering, AI engineering, data science, or computer science
Practical experience applying AI/ML/LLM technologies in real-world projects
Experience translating business needs into technical or product requirements
Strong communication skills with the ability to simplify complex AI concepts for non‑technical audiences
Experience delivering solutions in regulated or risk-aware environments (privacy, fairness, model behaviour) is a plus
Exposure to AI governance, AI literacy/change, AIOps/MLOps/LLMOps, model evaluation, or data privacy considerations (desirable)
Hands-on experience with low-code and automation platforms e.g., Microsoft Power Automate, Copilot Studio (desirable)
